dailyLog.description: Keep short daily notes beside the rest of your health record.
dailyLog.emptyDescription: Add a note, activity, or exposure when it is useful context for your records.
appShell.promptPlaceholder: Ask about a symptom, result, or trend
appShell.startDesktop: Start the desktop app to load local records.
fasting.safety.title: Check that fasting is appropriate for you
fasting.safety.description: Do not fast without clinician guidance if you are pregnant or breastfeeding, have a history of an eating disorder, take glucose-lowering medication, have recurrent low blood sugar, or have kidney or liver disease. Stop if you feel faint, confused, shaky, persistently nauseated, or otherwise unwell.
fasting.description: Set a clear target, run the local timer, and keep a factual history of completed fasting windows.
fasting.timer.label: Fasting timer
fasting.timer.title: Current fast
fasting.timer.readyDescription: Choose a target, then start when your last calorie-containing food or drink is finished.
fasting.timer.targetReached: Target reached. Longer is not automatically better.
fasting.target.label: Fasting target
fasting.stages.title: Fasting stages
fasting.stages.variability: These zones are orientation points, not biological checkpoints. Meal size and composition, activity, sleep, medication, and individual metabolism change the timing.
fasting.stage.0.title: After your last meal
fasting.stage.0.description: Digestion and recently absorbed carbohydrate, fat, and protein remain major fuel sources.
fasting.stage.4.title: Post-meal transition
fasting.stage.4.description: The post-meal glucose and insulin response is usually declining. Meal size and composition strongly affect the timing.
fasting.stage.8.title: Overnight window
fasting.stage.8.description: This resembles a typical overnight interval. Liver glycogen continues helping maintain blood glucose between meals.
fasting.stage.12.title: Stored-fuel shift
fasting.stage.12.description: Fat use may rise as liver glycogen falls. The metabolic shift has no exact clock time and is not itself a health outcome.
fasting.stage.16.title: Time-restricted range
fasting.stage.16.description: A 16-hour fast is common in time-restricted eating studies. Research evaluates repeated schedules, not a guaranteed benefit from one session.
fasting.stage.18.title: Extended fasting
fasting.stage.18.description: A longer window does not guarantee more benefit. Medication, low-blood-sugar, hydration, and nutrition risks matter more as fasting extends.
fasting.history.title: Recent fasts
jobs.title: Background work
jobs.description: Document analysis and research tasks stay visible while you move through your records.
jobs.emptyDescription: Document analysis and deep research will appear here.
jobs.stopDiscarded: Stopped. Any result returned by the active call will be discarded.
jobs.cancelled: This task stopped after you switched databases.
jobs.interrupted: This task was interrupted when the desktop app closed.
jobs.aiChatDescription: Working from your approved health context
developer.title: Developer
developer.description: Inspect local request timing, Codex commands, and failure details while you test the app.
developer.privacy: Prompt text, extracted results, and secrets are excluded. Only bounded metadata and errors are stored locally.
developer.interrupted: The app closed before this Codex call finished.
developer.runs.title: Runs
developer.runs.description: Active and recent work. Stopping discards a response after the active call returns.
developer.runs.emptyDescription: Document analysis and AI conversations appear here while they run.
developer.diagnosticsDescription: Request details and local event history.
developer.calls.title: LLM calls
developer.calls.description: Each row represents a real Codex CLI invocation from this app.
developer.calls.emptyDescription: Start document analysis or an AI conversation to capture request details here.
developer.logs.title: Event log
developer.logs.description: Renderer stages and bounded error details, newest first.
developer.logs.emptyDescription: Document preparation and AI activity will appear here as you use the app.
desktop.requiredTail: The raw Vite URL is only an internal renderer for Tauri.

body.hero.label: Health attention summary
body.hero.inRangeDetail: Your tracked markers are in range. Keep logging results over time.

settings.developmentMockNotice: Development uses a plaintext synthetic mock database. Password controls are available only in release builds.

settings.profile.anatomyDescription: Choose the body illustration independently from demographic sex.

settings.ai.thinkingEffortDescription: Controls how long Codex thinks before responding.
settings.ai.codexModelsUnavailable: Using bundled Codex defaults until the CLI model catalog is available.
settings.ai.allowRemoteDescription: Keep off unless you explicitly want health context sent to the selected remote provider.
settings.ai.apiTokenLmStudioDescription: Stored with local AI settings. Do not use a real token in development; leave blank when LM Studio does not require auth.

settings.ai.baseUrlRequired: Enter a base URL before saving.

settings.export.description: Save a SQLCipher copy of the unlocked local database.

settings.export.ready: Passphrases match. The encrypted copy is ready to export.

gate.body.setup: Choose a passphrase before the app stores health records on this device.
gate.body.migration: We found an older local database. Encrypt it before opening the dashboard.

gate.passphraseTooShort: Enter at least 12 characters after leading and trailing spaces are removed.
chat.none: No conversations yet. Ask a question to start a saved thread.

documents.title: Documents
documents.description: Extract results from PDFs and images with Codex, or save a local Apple Health export summary.
documents.sourcesDescription: Choose a source below. Result files open a review step before anything is saved; Apple Health imports keep a local summary.

documents.pdfDescription: Drop a PDF or image. Codex extracts draft results for you to review and accept.
documents.appleDescription: Choose the unzipped export.xml file in the desktop app. This saves counts and date range only, not metrics.

documents.emptyAppleDescription: Import an export.xml file to keep a local summary beside your records.
documents.deleteAppleImportConfirm: Delete this Apple Health import summary?
diet.title: Diet
diet.description: Record what you ate and the context around it so meal patterns can be compared with symptoms, results, and routines.
diet.formDescription: Keep it factual. Detailed calorie estimates are optional and are not inferred by the app.
diet.notesPlaceholder: Timing, appetite, symptoms, or anything worth comparing later
diet.emptyDescription: Your first entry will appear here and in the health timeline.
research.title: Deep Research
research.description: Ask a focused question and get a detailed report that compares your complete dated health record with clear uncertainty and data gaps.
research.emptyDescription: Deep Research maps the dated evidence, then verifies a structured report against the original records. It does not browse the web or invent sources.
research.example.trends: Which results and symptoms have changed together over time?
research.example.preparation: What should I prepare for my next clinician appointment?
research.example.patterns: Are there patterns across diet, medication, sleep notes, and symptoms?
research.scopeDescription: Your current database has {count} saved records available for the next report.
research.privacy: Remote endpoints receive this context only when remote health context is enabled. Loopback OpenAI-compatible endpoints remain on this device.
research.placeholder: Ask a question about your complete health record
research.setupRequired: Choose a configured LLM in Settings. Remote endpoints also require health-context approval.
research.workingDescription: The selected LLM is comparing dated records, testing possible relationships, and identifying uncertainty.

challenges.title: Challenges
challenges.description: Define a time-bound routine you want to try, then keep the target and dates visible while you work through it.
challenges.formDescription: Keep the target concrete. This is a personal tracking tool, not a treatment plan.
challenges.name: Challenge name
challenges.measureDescription: Write how you will know the challenge is complete.
challenges.notesPlaceholder: Why this matters, what to watch, or what might get in the way
challenges.listDescription: Mark a challenge complete when you have finished the defined window.
challenges.emptyDescription: Create a small, time-bound routine and keep its measure specific enough to review later.
challenges.validation: Add a name and valid dates. The end date must be on or after the start date.
toast.providerPlanned: {provider} is not available for live chat.
toast.regimenDeleted: Regimen item deleted from active views.
toast.reportDeletedWithResults: Report and linked results deleted from active views.

charts.matrix.title: Follow-up priority matrix
charts.matrix.description: Scan saved follow-up priorities across recent result dates. Range position is available in each result's details.
history.summary.followUpDetail: Based on the follow-up priority saved with each marker's latest result.
nav.breathing.label: Breathing
breathing.title: Breathing practice
breathing.description: Use breathwork as a brief reset, not as a treatment. Stop and return to normal breathing if you feel dizzy, anxious, numb, or unwell.
breathing.complete: Round complete. Return to normal breathing and notice how you feel before continuing.
breathing.paced.title: Paced breathing
breathing.paced.safety: Keep the exhale comfortable. There are no breath holds in this option.
breathing.box.title: Box breathing
breathing.box.safety: Skip the holds or choose paced breathing if they feel uncomfortable.
breathing.wimHof.title: Wim Hof breathing
breathing.wimHof.safety: This is an intense hyperventilation-style practice. Do it seated or lying down, never in or near water, while driving, or when operating equipment. This guide stops before a breath hold.
breathing.wimHof.acknowledge: I am seated or lying down, not in or near water, and not operating a vehicle or equipment.
